gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Upper bound (inclusive) on every declared dimension field of a [`Repr`] — `width`, `trits`,"] # [doc = " `dim`, and a [`SparsityClass::Sparse`] `max_active`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Why a cap at all (input-validation / DoS guard, DN-40 §3).** The wire forms (`repr.schema.json`,"] # [doc = " `value.schema.json`) carry these as `u32`, so a crafted descriptor can declare a dimension up to"] # [doc = " `u32::MAX` (≈ 4.29 × 10⁹). Materializing a value of such a `Repr` allocates that many elements"] # [doc = " (e.g. an `f64` `Hypervector`/`Scalars` vector), so an unbounded declared dimension is a latent"] # [doc = " over-allocation (denial-of-service) vector on the deserialize path. The lower `> 0` guard alone"] # [doc = " does not close it."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Why `2^30` (1 073 741 824).** It is a generous-but-finite ceiling that no legitimate value"] # [doc = " needs: VSA hypervectors are typically ~10⁴, dense embeddings ≤ ~10⁵, and bit/trit widths far"] # [doc = " smaller — all orders of magnitude under the cap. At the same time a `Repr` at the cap is already"] # [doc = " impractical to materialize (a `2^30`-element `f64` vector is 8 GiB), so anything above it is"] # [doc = " firmly in DoS territory, never a real workload. A power of two keeps the constant auditable"] # [doc = " (KC-3). The *check* is **`Exact`** — a declared dimension is either within the cap or it is not,"] # [doc = " a total decidable predicate; the *cap value* `2^30` is itself a **`Declared`** policy choice (a"] # [doc = " DoS heuristic, not an `Exact` fact about any value). The rejection is never-silent —"] # [doc = " [`Repr::check_well_formed`] returns [`WfError::DimensionTooLarge`] naming the offending field,"] # [doc = " its value, and this cap (G2)."] pub const MAX_DIM : u32 = 1 << 30 ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const MAX_DIM` — initializer is not a decidable non-negative integer literal or a known workspace-floor path (e.g. `RecursionBudget::DEFAULT_DEPTH_LIMIT`); no const item production in the grammar, and co-emit refuses to invent a value (ORACLE-R1 A4 / VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The four closed paradigm kinds (RFC-0001 §4.1)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq , Serialize , Deserialize)] # [serde (tag = "kind")] pub enum Repr { # [doc = " `n`-bit value."] Binary { # [doc = " Bit width (`> 0` when well-formed)."] width : u32 , } , # [doc = " `m` balanced trits in `{-1, 0, +1}`."] Ternary { # [doc = " Trit count (`> 0` when well-formed)."] trits : u32 , } , # [doc = " `dim`-dimensional dense embedding of the given scalar precision."] Dense { # [doc = " Dimensionality (`> 0` when well-formed)."] dim : u32 , # [doc = " Element precision (semantically significant — bounds embedding error)."] dtype : ScalarKind , } , # [doc = " Hypervector of the named VSA model."] # [serde (rename = "VSA")] Vsa { # [doc = " Model id, resolved against the VSA registry (ADR-008); non-empty when well-formed."] model : String , # [doc = " Hypervector dimensionality (`> 0` when well-formed)."] dim : u32 , # [doc = " Declared sparsity class."] sparsity : SparsityClass , } , # [doc = " A first-class indexed homogeneous sequence (RFC-0032 D3; M-749). `len` elements, each of the"] # [doc = " element representation `elem`. The substrate for an O(1)-indexed `Vec`/`Map`/`Set` (the"] # [doc = " efficient collections surface) — distinct from the recursive-ADT cons-`List`, which needs no"] # [doc = " kernel support. Unlike the scalar paradigms' dimension fields, **`len == 0` is well-formed**"] # [doc = " (the empty sequence is a legitimate value); only the [`MAX_DIM`] over-allocation cap and the"] # [doc = " nested `elem`'s own well-formedness gate it (never-silent on a malformed element repr, G2)."] Seq { # [doc = " The (boxed) element representation — every element of the payload matches this `Repr`."] elem : Box < Repr > , # [doc = " Declared element count (`≤ MAX_DIM` when well-formed; `0` is allowed — the empty seq)."] len : u32 , } , # [doc = " A first-class scalar IEEE-754 float of the given frozen-tag width (ADR-040 §2.1; M-896) —"] # [doc = " sibling to `Binary`/`Ternary`, **distinct from** [`Repr::Dense`]'s float *dtypes* (tensor"] # [doc = " storage formats, RFC-0033 §4.3.2): no implicit scalar↔rank-0-tensor identification."] # [doc = " Arithmetic semantics (RNE-only, in-band IEEE specials) are carried by *operations*"] # [doc = " (M-898+), never by this descriptor — the ADR-028 parallel (ADR-040 §2.2)."] Float { # [doc = " The frozen-tag width ([`FloatWidth::F64`] only at introduction — ADR-040 FLAG-1)."] width : FloatWidth , } , # [doc = " A first-class byte string (RFC-0032 D4; M-750) — **well-formed for any byte content**,"] # [doc = " carrying no declared length (the payload [`crate::value::Payload::Bytes`] carries the bytes)."] # [doc = " Text layers on top: UTF-8 decode is written in `.myc` over this byte surface, never in the"] # [doc = " kernel. The substrate for an efficient `str`/`text` value, chosen over modelling strings as"] # [doc = " `Seq<Binary{8}>` so text has a clear first-class value."] Bytes , }
gaps.reason: enum `Repr` has a named-field variant referencing a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Check one dimension field against the `> 0` lower guard and the [`MAX_DIM`] upper guard,"] # [doc = " returning a never-silent [`WfError::DimensionTooLarge`] (naming `field`, the value, and the cap)"] # [doc = " when the cap is exceeded, or `Ok(false)` when the value is non-positive (the caller maps that to"] # [doc = " [`WfError::MalformedRepr`], preserving the existing `> 0` contract). `Ok(true)` means in-range."] fn dim_in_range (field : & 'static str , value : u32) -> Result < bool , WfError > { if value == 0 { return Ok (false) ; } if value > MAX_DIM { return Err (WfError :: DimensionTooLarge { field , value , cap : MAX_DIM , }) ; } Ok (true) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Check a *length* field against the [`MAX_DIM`] over-allocation cap **only** — unlike"] # [doc = " [`dim_in_range`], `0` is accepted (a [`Repr::Seq`] of `len == 0` is the well-formed empty"] # [doc = " sequence, RFC-0032 D3). Returns the never-silent [`WfError::DimensionTooLarge`] (naming `field`,"] # [doc = " the value, and the cap) when the cap is exceeded; `Ok(())` otherwise. Same DoS guard as"] # [doc = " `dim_in_range`, without the `> 0` lower bound."] fn len_in_cap (field : & 'static str , value : u32) -> Result < () , WfError > { if value > MAX_DIM { return Err (WfError :: DimensionTooLarge { field , value , cap : MAX_DIM , }) ; } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: impl Repr { # [doc = " Well-formed iff all widths/dims/trits (and any `max_active`) are positive **and within"] # [doc = " [`MAX_DIM`]** and a VSA `model` id is non-empty — matching `repr.schema.json`"] # [doc = " (`minimum: 1` / `minLength: 1`) plus the over-allocation cap (DN-40 §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the `bool` predicate; [`Repr::check_well_formed`] is the never-silent variant that"] # [doc = " names *why* (used on the construction/deserialize path via [`crate::Value::new`])."] # [must_use] pub fn well_formed (& self) -> bool { self . check_well_formed () . is_ok () } # [doc = " Never-silent well-formedness check (G2): returns `Ok(())` when the descriptor is well-formed,"] # [doc = " [`WfError::DimensionTooLarge`] (naming the field, value, and [`MAX_DIM`]) when a declared"] # [doc = " dimension exceeds the over-allocation cap, or [`WfError::MalformedRepr`] for a non-positive"] # [doc = " dimension or empty VSA model id. Enforced on the construction/deserialize path through"] # [doc = " [`crate::Value::new`], so a crafted huge declared dimension is rejected *before* any value is"] # [doc = " materialized — closing the DN-40 §3 over-allocation gap."] pub fn check_well_formed (& self) -> Result < () , WfError > { let in_range = match self { Repr :: Binary { width } => dim_in_range ("width" , * width) ? , Repr :: Ternary { trits } => dim_in_range ("trits" , * trits) ? , Repr :: Dense { dim , .. } => dim_in_range ("dim" , * dim) ? , Repr :: Vsa { model , dim , sparsity , } => { let dim_ok = dim_in_range ("dim" , * dim) ? ; let sparsity_ok = match sparsity { SparsityClass :: Dense => true , SparsityClass :: Sparse { max_active } => { dim_in_range ("max_active" , * max_active) ? } } ; dim_ok && ! model . is_empty () && sparsity_ok } Repr :: Seq { elem , len } => { len_in_cap ("len" , * len) ? ; elem . check_well_formed () ? ; true } Repr :: Float { .. } => true , Repr :: Bytes => true , } ; if in_range { Ok (()) } else { Err (WfError :: MalformedRepr) } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (2 sub-issue(s)): impl method `well_formed` body: method call `.is_ok(...)` has no proven-emitted free-fn referent in this file (neither a D4-mangled inherent `Type__is_ok` nor a bare inherent recorded by this file's left-to-right pass, nor a prim_map / combinator row). Emitting bare `is_ok(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered); impl method `check_well_formed` body: struct-variant pattern `Binary_kw { .. }` names a constructor with no confirmed in-file layout -- resolved only for an emitted, in-file struct (or, once DN-132's SS5.1 variant-aware `StructLayout` population lands, an in-file enum's named-field variant); never a guessed field-index arity (DN-132 SS5.1/OQ-4, VR-5/G2)
